Technical Authority

Core Competencies

01
Process Optimization & Automation

Expert in optimizing complex manufacturing systems, deploying advanced automation technologies, and integrating precision workflows to enhance efficiency, reduce waste, and drive measurable operational improvements.

02
Product Development

Specialized in designing and developing innovative products, implementing robust engineering solutions, and managing end-to-end development workflows to ensure high quality, efficiency, and market-ready performance.

03
Continuous Improvement

Expert in identifying process inefficiencies, implementing data-driven improvements, and applying lean methodologies to enhance productivity, reduce variability, and drive measurable operational excellence.

04
Simulation & Analytical Modeling

Proficient in developing high-fidelity simulations and analytical models, leveraging advanced computational tools to predict system behavior, optimize processes, and drive data-informed engineering decisions.

Selected Work

Projects · Performance · Impact

Selected Professional Projects
1 / 5
Battery Voltage Test Automation

Developed and implemented a fully automated voltage testing machine for the battery finishing line in response to Control Engineering team requirements; created concept, performed CAD design, sourced vendor quotations, and managed installation, eliminating manual testing while increasing throughput, improving measurement accuracy, and reducing labor dependency.

1 / 5
Mold Changeover Optimization

Led and executed a complete new mold setup for production changeovers; developed concept, performed CAD design and layout, sourced quotations, and managed installation on the production line, reducing changeover time from 5 hours to 2 hours (60% improvement) and significantly increasing line availability and operational efficiency.

1 / 6
NPI from Concept to Launch

Led cross-functional engineering teams through full-cycle product development — from concept ideation and market research to 3D/2D CAD prototyping, mechanical and electro-mechanical component design, and validation — ensuring compliance with functional, aesthetic, regulatory, and manufacturability requirements while optimizing performance and cost, successfully launching multiple new products on time and within budget.

1 / 6
Refrigerator Compressor Housing

Led the end-to-end product development of a new compressor housing, including concept creation, detailed CAD modeling, and engineering validation. Conducted comprehensive FEA-based stress analysis to ensure structural integrity, durability, and manufacturability. Collaborated closely with cross-functional teams to design and implement a common sheet-metal die, reducing manufacturing complexity and improving part standardization across product lines.

1 / 7
Refrigerator Door

Led the complete development of a new refrigerator door for an upcoming model, from research to production readiness. Conducted competitive benchmarking and market research to identify performance gaps, consumer expectations, and design opportunities. Designed the full CAD model and collaborated with mold designers to ensure manufacturability.

1 / 10
Refrigerator Duct

Developed a new refrigerator air-duct system through competitive benchmarking and airflow analysis. Created the complete CAD model and optimized duct geometry for improved efficiency and performance. Collaborated with both international and in-house mold designers to ensure manufacturability and optimize tooling for high-volume production. Partnered closely with cross-functional teams to refine design feasibility and production readiness.

1 / 4
Refrigerator Back Sheet

Developed the refrigerator back sheet by collaborating with die makers and cross-functional teams to create a common, standardized die for production. Performed detailed FEA stress analysis to validate structural strength and durability under operating loads. Generated complete CAD models and supported design optimization to improve manufacturability.

Academic Projects
Master's Project
1 / 5
Crankshaft Mass Reduction via FEA

This study performs Finite Element Analysis (FEA) on a crankshaft to reduce its mass while maintaining safety factors. Various materials, including cast iron and steel alloys, are evaluated, with cast iron preferred for its high stress-bearing capacity. Using MATLAB verification and ABAQUS simulations, four design models are tested by reducing the main journal diameter, crankpin diameter, and crank web thickness. Results show a 20% weight reduction is achievable without compromising structural safety.

Bachelor's Thesis
1 / 7
Magnetic Levitation with PID Control

A small magnetic levitation system was designed and constructed, with position control achieved using a single-degree-of-freedom analog PID controller designed via pole assignment. Experiments and simulations demonstrate that the suspended object remains nearly stable under the applied control.

About Me

Focused · Disciplined · Leader

Md Ariful Azam Arif
MSME · LSSGB · Process Engineer
Location
Iowa, USA
Current Role
Process Engineer, Stryten Energy
Phone
(618) 917-7312
Email
[email protected]
LinkedIn
View Profile →

I am Md Ariful Azam Arif, widely known as Arif, a Mechanical Engineer from Bangladesh whose career reflects determination, global ambition, and a strong foundation in product development, manufacturing, and process engineering.

Born and raised in Bangladesh, I earned my Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering from Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology (RUET) — one of the country's best engineering institutions. During my undergraduate years, I developed a strong understanding of manufacturing systems, mechanical design, thermodynamics, and industrial operations. RUET shaped my analytical mindset and strengthened my ability to approach complex engineering challenges with structured, data-driven solutions.

After graduation, I began my professional journey at Walton Group, one of the largest manufacturing companies in Bangladesh. There, I gained hands-on experience in high-volume production environments, industrial processes, and manufacturing optimization. Working in a fast-paced setting helped me build practical expertise in product development, efficiency enhancement, and cross-functional collaboration — laying the foundation for my career in advanced manufacturing.

Driven by a desire to expand my technical expertise and gain global exposure, I moved to the United States to pursue my Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville (SIUE). While balancing academic rigor with real-world application, I actively pursued industry experience to bridge theory with practice.

During my graduate studies, I interned at Amsted Rail, where I worked within precision-driven manufacturing systems and quality-focused production environments. I later joined Battle Motors as an intern, gaining experience in electric vehicle manufacturing and process optimization within a rapidly evolving industry. These experiences strengthened my technical versatility and broadened my understanding of both traditional and next-generation manufacturing systems.

Currently, I work as a Process Engineer at Stryten Energy, where I focus on production optimization, takt time analysis, process improvement, SOP development, and continuous improvement initiatives. I apply lean manufacturing principles, data-driven decision-making, and engineering fundamentals to enhance operational efficiency and quality performance. My role involves implementing scalable process improvements, collaborating closely with QA and production teams, and developing standardized systems that drive consistency and measurable results.

My journey — from Bangladesh to the United States — reflects resilience, adaptability, and a commitment to continuous growth. With experience across multinational manufacturing environments — ranging from consumer electronics to rail systems, electric vehicles, and energy storage — I strive to deliver operational excellence while continuously learning, improving, and innovating.
